model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"Decoupled weight decay with AdamW usually improves short-budget MLP generalization on CIFAR-10, and a modestly stronger decay can reduce overfitting without destabilizing 256-step training.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 7e-4","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"With warmup+cosine and AdamW, a moderately higher peak learning rate often improves progress in a short 256-step budget for MLPs without adding complexity.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 7e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The prior LR increase produced a strong gain, so the next highest-value probe is another LR step-up to test whether this short-budget regime is still under-driven.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Loss kept improving as LR increased, so a smaller follow-up increase is a high-probability way to capture additional short-horizon optimization gains without extra complexity.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    warmup_steps = max(int(0.05 * total_steps), 1)","old":"    warmup_steps = max(int(0.1 * total_steps), 1)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"At the now-higher learning rate, shortening warmup should spend more of the 256-step budget near peak LR, which can improve convergence speed in this constrained regime.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    warmup_steps = max(int(0.1 * total_steps), 1)","old":"    warmup_steps = max(int(0.05 * total_steps), 1)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"The shorter warmup regressed validation loss, so revert to the prior 10% warmup that aligned with the best observed configuration.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.4e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Performance has improved across successive LR increases, so a small additional increase is the most direct next probe for further short-budget loss reduction.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.6e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.4e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Incremental LR increases continue to improve validation loss, so the next efficient experiment is another small LR step-up while keeping the rest fixed.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.8e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.6e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The strongest gains are still coming from incremental LR increases, so the next high-value low-complexity step is another small LR raise under the same warmup+cosine schedule.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 14","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"After multiple LR gains, the next likely bottleneck is capacity; a small width increase should improve fit while keeping optimization behavior and code simplicity unchanged.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 14","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The width increase caused a large regression under the fixed-step budget, indicating the extra capacity is undertrained; revert to the better-performing smaller model.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.8e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"LR scaling has been consistently beneficial, so testing 2e-3 is a minimal next step to check if the optimum is slightly higher in this fixed 256-step setting.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The validation loss is still improving with higher LR, so a controlled step to 2.2e-3 is a strong next probe for additional short-budget optimization gain.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.2e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The move to 2.2e-3 slightly regressed validation loss, so reverting to 2e-3 restores the current best-performing learning-rate setting.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 3e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"With stronger learning rates and only 256 steps, slightly reducing weight decay can improve early fitting and lower validation loss before over-regularization dominates.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 2e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 3e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The small gain from lowering weight decay suggests slight over-regularization remains; another modest reduction is a targeted low-risk follow-up.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 2e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Weight-decay reductions are still giving slight gains, so testing 1e-4 is a simple final-range probe for the best regularization strength under this short training budget.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"With only 256 optimizer steps, lowering AdamW beta2 can improve responsiveness of second-moment estimates and sometimes yields better early-stage convergence.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.98)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The beta2 reduction to 0.99 improved results; one final small reduction to 0.98 is a targeted short-horizon optimization tweak that may further improve adaptation within 256 steps.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}
